summary: Mobile experience is degraded across the board: the kanban board has no card-stack fallback on 375px, modals overflow on tablet (max-w breaks the shadcn sm:max-w gotcha pattern), confirm() dialogs and missing aria-labels block screen readers, and 'Pipeline' branding still leaks into user-facing copy where 'Content Engine' should appear.

findings.title: Kanban board has no mobile card-stack — 5 columns force horizontal scroll at 375px
findings.evidence:
<div className="flex gap-3 p-4 overflow-x-auto min-h-0 flex-1 snap-x snap-mandatory md:snap-none touch-pan-x">
  {PIPELINE_COLUMNS.map((column) => (
    <PipelineColumn key={column.id} column={column} cards={...} />
  ))}
findings.user_impact: On phone, the core workflow (the Content Engine board) renders as a horizontal swipe carousel of 240px columns. Users can only see one column at a time, never the whole pipeline. There's no stage filter or accordion fallback. The hard rule from project memory ('tables → card-stack on mobile') is violated for the central screen of the product.
findings.repro: Open smm.ten80ten.com on iPhone Safari (375px). Click Content Engine. Observe: only one column fits; must swipe four times to reach Posted; no jump-to-stage UI; column header still tiny.
findings.fix:
Add a mobile-only stage tab strip above the lane and render only the selected stage as a vertical stack. Wrap the existing column grid in `hidden md:flex` and add a new `` that maps `PIPELINE_COLUMNS` into a horizontal scroll of pills (44px tap targets) and renders a single `` full-width below. Example: `
{PIPELINE_COLUMNS.map(c => )}
`.

findings.title: Dashboard auto-scales below 1 on desktop using transform — breaks click targets and pinches text
findings.user_impact: On laptops with limited vertical room (13-inch MacBook, browser dev tools open) the entire dashboard CSS-scales down to fit. Text becomes blurry, buttons get smaller than 44px, hit boxes shift, hover ring is offset. Worse, the `width: 100/scale%` blows the layout past the viewport horizontally on some scale values.
findings.repro: Open dashboard at 1024×600 height. Observe scale<1 applied. Hover the 'Review Posts' button — focus ring renders at original coords, not scaled. Inspector reports text-blur from non-integer scale.
findings.fix: Drop the auto-fit scale entirely; use proper responsive density and `overflow-y-auto` instead. Replace lines 50-71 with simple flex column. Mobile already uses `sm:overflow-hidden` which is wrong direction. Instead make the page `overflow-y-auto` everywhere and let users scroll like a normal page.

findings.title: Delete post uses native confirm() dialog — unstyled, blocks UI, no keyboard trap
findings.evidence: <button onClick={() => { if (confirm("Delete this post?")) { deleteCard(selectedCard.id); closeDrawer(); } }} ...>
findings.user_impact: Destructive action uses the browser's native confirm() — appears in the wrong place on mobile (top of viewport on iOS), no warning copy about what's deleted, no 'cannot be undone', no way for user to verify they're deleting the right post. Inconsistent with the rest of the app's modal style.
findings.repro: Open any card → click trash icon → see browser-chrome confirm box.
findings.fix: Replace with a styled inline confirmation modal. Use the existing kickback-modal.tsx pattern but with destructive copy: 'Delete <post title>? This permanently removes the card and its history. Cannot be undone.' Two-step button: 'Type DELETE to confirm' for posts in approved_scheduled or posted (per migration 0015 hard rule).

findings.title: Settings 'Open Studio to all writer roles' uses confirm() for a permission grant
findings.evidence: if (!confirm("Open Studio to every writer role (admin, owner, creative_director, social_media_specialist)? You can re-lock anytime.")) return;
findings.user_impact: Admin grants org-wide AI generation access through a native confirm() with no count of affected users, no preview of who gets access, no audit warning. One click expands $3/row cost authority to up to 4 roles.
findings.repro: Settings → Creator Studio Access → click the 'Open to all writers' button.
findings.fix: Replace with a styled confirmation Dialog listing each role and the # of users in each, plus a 'Send notification email to affected users' checkbox. Reuse the validation-error-modal.tsx shell.

findings.title: Body locked to h-screen — iOS Safari URL bar overlaps footer, breaks h-dvh rule
findings.evidence: <div className="h-screen flex bg-[#fafbfc] dark:bg-[#0a0a0a] overflow-hidden">
findings.user_impact: On mobile Safari the address bar shows/hides on scroll, but `h-screen` (= 100vh) reserves space that doesn't account for the dynamic chrome. The footer copyright and CTA buttons get covered when the URL bar collapses. Project memory rule explicitly says use `h-dvh`.
findings.repro: Open on iPhone Safari. Scroll any page. Bottom 8px of footer is clipped by URL bar. Open keyboard in a textarea — modal scroll breaks.
findings.fix: Replace `h-screen` with `h-dvh` (Tailwind 4 supports it natively): `<div className="h-dvh flex bg-[#fafbfc] ...">`. Also update the desktop sidebar `h-screen` on line 281 to `h-dvh`. Same change needed in `min-h-screen` on auth pages (login-screen.tsx, request-access, forgot-password, reset-password, setup).

findings.title: Login copy says 'Sign in to your content pipeline' — internal jargon in public-facing screen
findings.evidence: Sign in to your content pipeline
findings.user_impact: Project memory IRON LAW: 'The product is called Ten80Ten Content Engine in all user-facing text'. 'Pipeline' is supposed to be internal-only. Login is the first thing every user sees. Also the right-panel decorative card on line 220 reads 'Pipeline' as a category label, and the bottom legend on line 297 reads 'Pipeline'.
findings.repro: Open / unauthenticated. Read the subhead under 'Welcome back'. See decorative analytics cards on right panel.
findings.fix: Change line 78 to 'Sign in to your Content Engine'. Change line 220 'Pipeline' label to 'Content Engine'. Change line 297 `{ label: "Pipeline", color: "bg-[#f59e0b]" }` to `{ label: "Engine", ... }` or `"Content"`.

findings.title: Auth right panel uses banned words 'Enterprise Secured' + 'dominate algorithms' + 'enterprise-grade'
findings.evidence:
Enterprise Secured ...

Deploy campaigns, manage approvals, and dominate algorithms from one master dashboard.
findings.user_impact: Project memory + CLAUDE.md banned words. 'Enterprise Secured' is dev/sales jargon, 'dominate algorithms' is AI-slop marketing speak, also appears in forgot-password.tsx:178+190 and reset-password.tsx:207+217. Banned per global rules. Aldridge will flag immediately.
findings.repro: Visit /, /auth/forgot-password, /auth/reset-password on desktop ≥1024px. Right panel renders all three.
findings.fix: Replace 'Enterprise Secured' → remove badge entirely or say 'Live status'. Replace 'dominate algorithms from one master dashboard' → 'Ship social campaigns, manage approvals, and post on schedule.' Replace 'enterprise-grade security system' → 'Reset your password and get back to work.' Replace 'enterprise-grade encryption' → 'Your password reset is encrypted in transit.'

findings.title: Modals lack ESC-key close and focus trap (only ValidationErrorModal handles ESC)
findings.evidence:
<div onClick={onClose} className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/30 dark:bg-black/60 z-50" />
<div className="fixed inset-0 flex items-center justify-center z-50 p-4">
  <div className="bg-white dark:bg-[#151518] ...">
findings.user_impact: Create Post, Kickback, Revision, Repurpose, AvatarCrop, MediaPicker, EditProfile, Lightbox — none close on ESC. Keyboard users (and accessibility audits) need a way out without clicking. Also no focus trap means Tab can escape the modal to the page underneath.
findings.repro: Open Create Post (cmd+click '+ Create Post' from sidebar). Press ESC. Modal stays open.
findings.fix: Extract a `useDialogKeyboard(open, onClose)` hook from validation-error-modal.tsx that wires up `Escape → onClose` and a focus trap. Apply to all `<div className="fixed inset-0 ... z-50">` patterns. Or just use the shadcn `Dialog` primitive which gives both for free.

findings.title: Sidebar auto-collapses every 6s on desktop — fights against user intent
findings.user_impact: Sidebar collapses by itself after 6 seconds if user hasn't pinned it. Users on first visit don't know about the pin and will see their nav 'disappear', causing confusion. Hover-to-expand works but the visual jolt is jarring. Notion/Linear/Attio don't auto-collapse.
findings.repro: Fresh desktop session. Don't touch sidebar. After 6s it collapses to 56px.
findings.fix: Default to collapsed (saves screen real estate, matches Linear), let user expand manually with persistent state. Drop the timer entirely. Or only auto-collapse if user hasn't interacted in 5 minutes, not 6 seconds.

findings.title: Top bar 'Page' is unreadable — hamburger is only nav indicator on mobile, no page title shown
findings.evidence:
<header className="h-12 flex items-center gap-2 px-4 border-b border-gray-100 ...">
  <button onClick={onMenuClick} aria-label="Open navigation menu" className="md:hidden p-2 -ml-1 rounded-lg ...">
    <Menu className="w-5 h-5" aria-hidden="true" />
  </button>
  <div className="flex-1" />
findings.user_impact: On mobile the top bar shows only [hamburger] [empty] [moon icon] [avatar]. The user can't tell what page they're on. Going from Pipeline → drag → modal → cancel — disorienting.
findings.repro: Open mobile. Tap hamburger → Content Calendar → close menu. Top bar gives no clue you're on Calendar vs Dashboard.
findings.fix:
Insert page title between the menu button and the spacer: `

{currentPage.replace('_', ' ')}

`. Use the navigation context's currentPage.

findings.title: Create Post modal max-h-[90vh] fails iOS Safari with dynamic toolbar — last field gets clipped
findings.evidence: <div className="bg-white dark:bg-[#151518] rounded-2xl border ... shadow-2xl w-full max-w-[580px] max-h-[90vh] flex flex-col">
findings.user_impact: On iOS Safari with keyboard open, the modal's `max-h-[90vh]` calculation uses the full viewport including hidden URL bar space, so the bottom 'Create Post' button gets pushed below the visible area. Same problem in revision-modal, kickback-modal, repurpose-modal.
findings.repro: Open Create Post on iPhone, focus the Caption field. Keyboard opens. Scroll inside modal. Submit button is unreachable.
findings.fix: Use `max-h-[90dvh]` instead of `max-h-[90vh]`. Tailwind 4 ships dynamic-viewport units. Apply to all modals: create-post-modal:269, repurpose-modal:155.

findings.title: Content card title click target = 220px wide × ~120px — entire card draggable, no separate click for open
findings.evidence:
<div ref={setNodeRef} style={style} {...attributes} {...listeners}
  onClick={() => !isDragging && selectCard(card)}
  className={`group rounded-xl overflow-hidden cursor-pointer ...`}>
findings.user_impact: On touch devices the whole card is both a drag handle and a click target. A 200ms touch delay (line 78) is the only differentiation. On phones, users routinely scroll the kanban lane and accidentally pick up cards. Combined with the dnd-kit accidental-drag, this means scrolling = dragging.
findings.repro: Mobile: scroll vertically through Ideas column. Cards 'lift' on scroll attempt. Sometimes drop into the wrong column.
findings.fix: Move drag listeners to a small explicit handle (top-left corner with a grip icon) instead of the whole card. Replace `{...attributes} {...listeners}` on the wrapper with a child `<button className="absolute top-1 left-1 p-1.5 -m-1.5" {...attributes} {...listeners}><GripVertical className="w-3 h-3" /></button>`. Card-tap stays for selectCard.

findings.title: Asset Review drawer takes full width on mobile but tab bar buttons are 11px font, easy to mistap
findings.evidence:
<div className="flex items-center gap-0.5 px-5 md:px-7 mx-4 md:mx-7 mb-2 mt-2 border-b ...">
  ...
  <button ... className="flex items-center gap-1.5 px-4 py-2.5 text-[11px] font-semibold ...">
findings.user_impact: Tab bar 'Content / Source Vault / Audit Trail' uses 11px font and 2.5 padding-y = ~34px tap height. Below the 44px minimum. On mobile fingers tap the wrong tab.
findings.repro: Open a card on iPhone. Try to tap 'Source Vault' tab while drawer is open. Often hits 'Content' or 'Audit Trail' instead.
findings.fix: Bump tab padding: `px-4 py-3 text-[12px]` and ensure min-height: `min-h-[44px]`. Also widen gap to `gap-1`.

findings.title: Studio cost cap chip is invisible on mobile — wraps to next line cut off, no contextual help
findings.evidence: ${spent.toFixed(2)} / ${cap.toFixed(2)} today
findings.user_impact: 10px font, no label. Shows '$0.42 / $10.00 today' but there's no explanation that this is the daily cap that stops generation. When pct hits 90% the chip turns red — user has no idea why their Generate buttons start failing.
findings.repro: Mobile. Generate 10+ rows. Chip flips amber → red. Generate fails silently. Confusion.
findings.fix:
Wrap chip in a tooltip or extend copy: `
Daily AI cap: ${spent.toFixed(2)} / ${cap.toFixed(2)}
` and on pct≥90 add a small banner above the row list: 'Daily AI cap nearly reached. Generation pauses at ${cap.toFixed(2)}.'

findings.title: Mention textarea dropdown positioned bottom-full — covers caption on small modal heights
findings.evidence: <div className="absolute left-0 bottom-full mb-1 w-64 max-h-72 overflow-y-auto bg-white dark:bg-[#1a1a1a] rounded-xl border ...">
findings.user_impact: Mention dropdown always pops UP from the textarea (bottom-full). In the comment composer at the BOTTOM of a drawer, this is fine. But in the kickback-modal/revision-modal at the TOP of the modal, the dropdown pops off-screen and is unreachable. Worse, the 64-unit fixed width (256px) is wider than mobile viewport (320px-360px content area) on iPhone SE / older Androids.
findings.repro: Open Kickback modal on iPhone SE. Type '@' in 'What needs to be fixed?'. Dropdown pops above and goes off-viewport / overlaps the heading.
findings.fix: Switch to floating-ui or compute position based on available space: `useState<'top'|'bottom'>(...)` with measurement. Or just use a Portal + popper. Quick fix: `w-full md:w-64 max-w-[90vw]` so it doesn't blow out mobile, and add `top-full mt-1` fallback when there's no room above (`window.innerHeight - rect.bottom < 280`).

findings.title: Calendar grid cells have min-height 100px — 35-cell month = 500px+ vertical on mobile, can't see the legend
findings.evidence:
<div key={i} className={`min-h-[100px] p-2 border-b border-r border-gray-50 dark:border-white/[0.03] ${
  !day ? "bg-gray-50/50 dark:bg-white/[0.01]" : "hover:bg-blue-50/30 dark:hover:bg-blue-500/[0.03]"
} transition-colors`}>
findings.user_impact: Mobile calendar grid forces 7 columns × min-height 100px. On 375px width, each cell is ~52px wide × 100px tall — too narrow to read the chip text. There's no list/agenda view fallback.
findings.repro: Open Content Calendar on iPhone. 7 cramped columns. Chips read 'IG TT 9:0...' with truncated titles. Each row scrolls forever.
findings.fix: Mobile-only agenda view: `<div className="md:hidden">` rendering scheduled cards as a vertical list grouped by date. Hide the grid via `<div className="hidden md:grid grid-cols-7">`. List items use the existing CalendarChip layout but full-width.

findings.title: Brand kit page tabs fixed at px-5 py-3 with 12px font — overflow on mobile and tap target tight
findings.evidence:
<div className="relative flex items-center gap-0.5 mt-6 border-b border-gray-200 dark:border-white/[0.06]">
  {TABS.map((tab) => (
    <button ... className="flex items-center gap-1.5 px-5 py-3 text-[12px] font-semibold ...">
findings.user_impact: 4 tabs × ~80px each = 320px which barely fits, and with `px-8` page padding the row overflows on iPhone SE. Buttons clip with no scrolling cue.
findings.repro: Open Brand Kit on iPhone SE. Tabs overflow off-screen. No way to reach 'Guardrails' without swiping (which has no visual affordance).
findings.fix: Add `overflow-x-auto -mx-8 px-8` to the tab container so it scrolls horizontally, and add visual scroll affordance: `<div className="absolute right-0 inset-y-0 w-8 bg-gradient-to-l from-white to-transparent pointer-events-none md:hidden" />`. Reduce padding on mobile: `px-3 sm:px-5`.

findings.title: Brand kit heading uses px-8 — content shoved off-screen on mobile, no responsive padding
findings.evidence:
...

Brand Playbook
findings.user_impact: Hardcoded px-8 (32px) × 2 = 64px horizontal padding. On 375px screen that's 17% wasted. Heading + Edit button row collapses awkwardly. Mobile rule: 'section padding scales down'.
findings.repro: Open Brand Kit on iPhone. Heading fits but Edit button wraps to next line, breaking the visual hierarchy.
findings.fix: Replace `px-8 pt-8` with `px-4 sm:px-8 pt-5 sm:pt-8`. Also update line 172 `px-8 py-8` to `px-4 sm:px-8 py-5 sm:py-8`.

findings.title: Top bar user menu dropdown clipped on mobile — fixed right-0 mt-1 w-48 cuts off screen
findings.evidence: <div className="absolute right-0 top-full mt-1 w-48 bg-white dark:bg-[#1a1a1a] rounded-xl border ... shadow-lg py-1 z-50">
findings.user_impact: On mobile the user menu (Settings / Profile / Sign Out) anchored right-0 width 192px sits very close to viewport edge. With dropdown items at 12px font + small icons, the email gets clipped: 'aldridge.dagos@ten8...'.
findings.repro: Mobile. Tap avatar in top bar. See dropdown. Email field is truncated mid-domain.
findings.fix: Increase to `w-56` and add `max-w-[calc(100vw-32px)]` so it never overflows. Also add `text-ellipsis overflow-hidden` on the email line so the truncation is intentional and dotted.

findings.title: Post Preview previews use fixed pixel widths (340/400/420/290) — break on mobile devices
findings.evidence:
<div className="w-[340px] bg-white rounded-xl overflow-hidden border border-gray-200 shadow-lg">
...
<div className="w-[400px] bg-white rounded-xl overflow-hidden border border-gray-200 shadow-lg">
...
<div className="w-[420px] bg-white rounded-xl overflow-hidden border border-gray-200 shadow-lg">
findings.user_impact: Instagram preview = 340px, Facebook = 400px, YouTube = 420px, LinkedIn = 420px, TikTok = 290px. On 375px screen the 400/420 previews overflow horizontally, get cut off, and don't scroll. Whole preview screen is unusable on mobile.
findings.repro: Open Post Preview on iPhone. Switch to LinkedIn or Facebook tab. Preview clips at right edge.
findings.fix: Wrap the preview in `<div className="max-w-full overflow-x-auto">` so it scrolls. Or better, make previews responsive: `<div className="w-full max-w-[400px]">` and let the platform-specific aspect ratios drive height. TikTok 290×500 should be `w-[min(290px,calc(100vw-32px))]`.

findings.title: Media Page sidebar collapses to top scroll bar on mobile — folder names overflow without scroll cue
findings.evidence: <div className="w-full md:w-[200px] border-b md:border-b-0 md:border-r border-gray-100 dark:border-white/[0.06] bg-white dark:bg-[#111] p-3 flex md:flex-col shrink-0 overflow-x-auto md:overflow-x-visible gap-1 md:gap-0">
findings.user_impact: On mobile, folder list renders as a horizontal scroll strip across the top. No visual cue that it scrolls. Folder pills like 'Pipeline Uploads' use `whitespace-nowrap` so they all sit in a row, but on first load only the first 3-4 show. Users miss the rest.
findings.repro: Mobile. Open Media Library with 6+ folders. Top strip looks like it ends after 'Free' → users don't realize they can swipe.
findings.fix: Add fade-out edge: `<div className="absolute right-0 top-0 bottom-0 w-8 bg-gradient-to-l from-white to-transparent pointer-events-none md:hidden" />` over the folder strip. Or change to a stage-bar pattern with a dropdown on mobile.

findings.title: MediaPicker has w-[280px] right detail panel — fixed width breaks small modals on tablet
findings.evidence: <div className="w-[280px] border-l border-gray-100 dark:border-white/[0.06] bg-gray-50/50 dark:bg-white/[0.02] p-4 overflow-y-auto shrink-0 space-y-4">
findings.user_impact: When user selects an asset in the Library tab on tablet, a 280px detail panel slides in from the right inside the picker modal. The modal itself is `inset-4 md:inset-8 lg:inset-y-12 lg:inset-x-[12%]` which can give a content area of ~600px on tablet. 280px panel + grid leaves ~250px for the asset grid — pictures shrink to thumbnails.
findings.repro: iPad portrait. Open Create Post → Browse Library → tap any asset. Grid items collapse to two tiny tiles per row.
findings.fix: On tablet stack the detail panel as a bottom drawer instead of a side panel: `<div className="w-full md:w-[280px] md:border-l border-t md:border-t-0 ...">`. Or hide the grid when an asset is selected on small screens: `<div className={selectedAsset ? 'hidden md:block flex-1 ...' : 'flex-1 ...'}>`.

findings.title: Skeleton card only renders 2 placeholders — full board (5 columns) feels empty on first load
findings.user_impact: On a slow connection or after login, each of the 5 columns shows only 2 skeleton placeholders. Looks like the board is empty + broken. With realistic counts (10+ in Ideas, 5 in Awaiting Approval) the skeleton-to-real transition is jarring.
findings.repro: Throttle network to 'Slow 3G'. Open / Content Engine. See sparse 2-per-column skeletons.
findings.fix: Render 3-6 skeletons per column based on viewport: `{Array.from({ length: 4 }).map((_, i) => <SkeletonCard key={i} />)}`. Add subtle shimmer animation to draw the eye.

findings.title: Settings invite/EditProfile modal has no max-w sm:max-w bake — sm screens silently clamp to 420px
findings.evidence: <div className="bg-white dark:bg-[#151518] rounded-2xl border border-gray-200 dark:border-white/[0.08] shadow-2xl w-full max-w-[420px]">
findings.user_impact: Project memory `reference_shadcn_dialog_sm_max_w_default`: shadcn DialogContent's `sm:max-w-sm` (384px) baked into default cva clamps width on `sm` breakpoint unless explicitly overridden. EditProfile uses `max-w-[420px]` only — works for non-shadcn modals but at sm: it would clamp if migrated to shadcn Dialog. Pre-emptive fix needed if migrating to shadcn Dialog primitive.
findings.repro: Migrate this modal to shadcn `<Dialog>`. Watch it silently clamp to 384px on `sm:` breakpoint.
findings.fix: When using shadcn Dialog, ALWAYS specify `sm:max-w-[420px]` explicitly when overriding default max-w. Inline-comment the duplicate so future audits don't strip it: `className="max-w-[420px] sm:max-w-[420px] /* explicit override of shadcn sm:max-w-sm default */"`.

findings.title: Drag-and-drop has no keyboard accessibility — KeyboardSensor missing from useSensors
findings.user_impact: dnd-kit ships a `KeyboardSensor` that enables space-to-pickup + arrow-keys-to-move + screen reader announcements via the built-in `Announcements` system. Currently keyboard users can't move cards at all — locking out screen-reader users entirely.
findings.repro: Tab through the board. Focus a card. Press space. Nothing happens.
findings.fix: Add `import { KeyboardSensor, sortableKeyboardCoordinates } from '@dnd-kit/core'; import { sortableKeyboardCoordinates } from '@dnd-kit/sortable';` and include in sensors: `useSensor(KeyboardSensor, { coordinateGetter: sortableKeyboardCoordinates })`. Then add accessibility props: `<DndContext accessibility={{ announcements: { onDragStart, onDragMove, ... } }}>`.

findings.title: Settings page tabs don't horizontally scroll on mobile — overflow clipped
findings.evidence:
<div className="flex items-center gap-1 border-b border-gray-100 dark:border-white/[0.06]">
  {[...].map((tab) => (
    <button ... className="flex items-center gap-1.5 px-4 py-2.5 text-[12px] ...">
findings.user_impact: Settings has 4 tabs: General / Team Members / Themes / Audit Logs. Plus a count badge and a pending-requests pulse dot. Total width pushes past 375px. No scroll, no wrap — last tab gets clipped.
findings.repro: Mobile. /settings. Audit Logs tab clipped on right.
findings.fix: Add `overflow-x-auto -mx-5 px-5` to the tab strip wrapper. Or stack: on mobile use a select/dropdown for tab choice.

findings.title: Toast container fixed top-right max-w-sm — toasts overlap top bar on mobile
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed top-4 right-4 z-[100] flex flex-col gap-2 max-w-sm">
findings.user_impact: On mobile (375px wide), `max-w-sm` (384px) — toast can render full-width minus 16px gutters. But anchored to `top-4` and the top bar is also at top (`h-12` = 48px). Toast covers the user menu / theme toggle, blocking interaction.
findings.repro: Mobile. Trigger any toast (e.g. log in). Toast covers the avatar and theme toggle buttons. Cannot dismiss menu until toast fades.
findings.fix: Anchor toasts to bottom on mobile: `bottom-4 right-4 md:top-4 md:bottom-auto`. Also reduce max width on mobile: `max-w-[calc(100vw-32px)] md:max-w-sm`. Sticky CTAs pattern requires bottom on mobile anyway.

findings.title: Asset Review drawer h-full + slide-from-right gives no scroll on mobile when long form has 1500+ px of content
findings.evidence: <div className="fixed right-0 top-0 bottom-0 w-full max-w-full md:max-w-[560px] z-50 flex flex-col bg-white dark:bg-[#0e0e11] border-l-0 md:border-l ...">
findings.user_impact: Drawer top-0 bottom-0 = 100% viewport. On phone with the URL bar visible, that's ~640px. Drawer has Identity + Media + Caption + Compliance + Notes + Checklist + Footer = easily 2000px. Footer is shrink-0 (line 987), so all content scrolls — but footer overlaps content when keyboard opens.
findings.repro: iPhone. Open any card. Tap into Notes textarea. Keyboard pops. Footer 'Submit Revision Request' / 'Move to ...' button gets covered by keyboard.
findings.fix: Change top-0 bottom-0 to `top-0 bottom-0` with `h-dvh` semantics + footer `pb-[env(safe-area-inset-bottom)]`. Or use a CSS approach where the active textarea scrolls into view on focus: `useEffect(() => textareaRef.current?.scrollIntoView({block: 'center'}), [keyboardOpen])`.

findings.title: Studio CompactSelect uses native <select> — iOS picker overlays content, no styling
findings.evidence:
<select value={value} disabled={disabled} onChange={(e) => onChange(e.target.value)}
  className="appearance-none h-8 w-full pl-2.5 pr-7 text-[12px] rounded-md border ...">
findings.user_impact: Native iOS picker pops up as a half-screen wheel. Studio has 4 of these per row (Media / Format / Slides / Aspect). Stack of native pickers = mobile workflow nightmare. No way to power-type to a value.
findings.repro: Studio on iPhone. Tap Media dropdown. Wheel picker covers all other fields. Cannot see the chip preview.
findings.fix: Use the existing PlatformDropdown pattern (custom button + dropdown) for these selects. Or wrap native select with a `pointer-events-none` overlay and use the custom dropdown when `window.matchMedia('(hover: none)').matches`.

findings.title: InlineEdit field has no visible 'editing' affordance — looks identical to read mode until clicked
findings.evidence:
<Tag onClick={() => setEditing(true)}
  className={`cursor-text rounded-lg px-3 py-2 -mx-3 -my-2 transition-all duration-150 hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-white/[0.03] hover:ring-1 hover:ring-gray-200 ...`}>
findings.user_impact: Editable post title and caption show a hover ring on desktop but on mobile (no hover) there's zero affordance. Users don't realize the title is editable. Project memory: 'don't make users guess what's clickable'.
findings.repro: Mobile. Open card. Tap title — works but no indication beforehand. Compared to Notion's pencil-icon-on-tap pattern.
findings.fix: On mobile, show a subtle pencil icon on the right edge: `<Pencil className="absolute right-2 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-3 h-3 text-gray-300 md:hidden" />` inside the wrapper.

findings.title: Avatar crop modal min-h-[300px] crop area leaves controls off-screen on landscape mobile
findings.evidence:
<div className="flex-1 relative bg-gray-900 min-h-[300px]">
  <Cropper image={imageSrc} crop={crop} zoom={zoom} aspect={1} cropShape="round" ... />
findings.user_impact: On landscape iPhone (375×667 rotated to 667×375), the modal occupies `inset-4` = 367×335. Crop area `flex-1 min-h-[300px]` eats 300px of 335 leaving 35px for header + controls + 'Cancel/Apply' buttons. Submit button hidden.
findings.repro: iPhone landscape. Open settings → edit profile → upload photo → crop. Cannot see Apply button.
findings.fix: Reduce min-h on landscape: `min-h-[200px] sm:min-h-[300px]`. Or detect landscape orientation and switch to side-by-side layout: `<div className="flex flex-col landscape:flex-row">` with controls on the right.

findings.title: Setup password page profile-photo button shows 'Click to upload' on mobile but tap target is only 80×80
findings.evidence: <div className="w-20 h-20 rounded-full border-2 border-dashed border-gray-300 dark:border-white/[0.12] group-hover:border-orange-400 dark:group-hover:border-orange-500 transition-colors flex items-center justify-center overflow-hidden bg-gray-50 dark:bg-white/[0.04]">
findings.user_impact: Profile photo upload button is 80×80px (5rem). Below 44px minimum visual ID and well below comfortable tap. Form requires photo. New user is forced to tap a small disk.
findings.repro: Open invite link on mobile. Try to tap the dashed avatar circle. Easy to miss; sometimes triggers behind elements.
findings.fix: Bump to `w-24 h-24 sm:w-20 sm:h-20` and make the surrounding label clickable too: ensure the parent `<label className="cursor-pointer group p-3">` adds padding so the effective hit area is ~120×120.

findings.title: Calendar 'Show less' / '+N more' buttons inside cell rely on hover state — no obvious affordance
findings.evidence:
<button onClick={() => toggleCell(i)}
  className="text-[10px] text-blue-500 hover:text-blue-600 font-medium px-2 cursor-pointer">
  +{hiddenCount} more
</button>
findings.user_impact: 10px font, blue underline-less. Easy to miss. Mobile users have no hover. The cell-content swallows the click sometimes.
findings.repro: Add 4+ posts to one day. Open Calendar. Find '+1 more' text — small and low contrast.
findings.fix: Make button a proper pill: `inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 py-1 rounded-full bg-blue-50 hover:bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-500/10 text-[10px] text-blue-700 font-bold cursor-pointer`. Add an icon: `<ChevronDown className="w-2.5 h-2.5" />`.

findings.title: Dashboard 'Welcome back' card stacks badly on mobile when name is long — overflows onto avatar
findings.evidence: Welcome back, {currentUser.name.split(" ")[0]}
findings.user_impact: Greeting uses 17px tracking-tight. For long first names ('Alexandros', 'Maximilian'), the line wraps awkwardly. Below: alert with 'You have N posts awaiting approval' — uses `mt-1 flex items-center gap-1.5` so the icon + text + count can't shrink and end up overlapping into 2 lines on small screens.
findings.repro: Mobile. User with 9+ letter first name + 2 pending approvals. 'You have 2 posts awaiting approval' wraps under the alert icon.
findings.fix:
Add `flex-wrap` on the alert line: `

`. Or stack the icon as block on mobile.

findings.title: Initial scale + max-scale set to 1 in viewport — blocks pinch-zoom for low-vision users
findings.user_impact: WCAG 1.4.4 violation: `maximumScale: 1` prevents low-vision users from pinch-zooming the page. iOS users can override system-wide but Android users with smaller phones can't read 8-10px stage labels and dashboard counts.
findings.repro: Mobile. Try to pinch-zoom any page. Browser blocks the gesture.
findings.fix: Remove `maximumScale: 1`. Tailwind's `text-[8px]` etc. mean users will want to zoom. The 'no zoom' pattern is only useful for game-like UIs.

findings.title: Login screen autocomplete attrs missing on email + password fields
findings.evidence:
<input
  type="email"
  placeholder="you@company.com"
  value={email}
  onChange={(e) => { setEmail(e.target.value); setError(""); }}
  className="..."
  autoFocus
/>
findings.user_impact: iOS keychain and 1Password can't autofill correctly without `autoComplete="email"` and `autoComplete="current-password"`. Users have to manually type.
findings.repro: iPhone with iCloud Keychain saved login. Visit /. Email field doesn't show the autofill suggestion above the keyboard.
findings.fix: Add `autoComplete="email"` to email input, `autoComplete="current-password"` to password input. Same fix for forgot-password (autocomplete="email"), reset-password (autocomplete="new-password"), setup (autocomplete="new-password" on both password fields, autocomplete="name" on First/Last Name, autocomplete="tel" on WhatsApp).

findings.title: Footer copyright text-[10px] gray-300 on white — fails WCAG AA contrast
findings.evidence: Ten80Ten Social Media Management Platform © 2026
findings.user_impact: gray-300 (#d1d5db) on white is 1.94:1 contrast — fails AA (4.5:1 minimum). Decorative? Sure. But same low-contrast pattern repeats throughout app for hints, metadata, timestamps — making the UI feel dusty.
findings.repro: Open Lighthouse a11y audit. See contrast warning on footer.
findings.fix: Bump to `text-gray-400` minimum (#9ca3af = 2.85:1, still low but at least visible). For text intended to be read, use `text-gray-500` (#6b7280 = 4.6:1).

findings.title: DragOverlay rotates 2deg + scale 1.05 — overlay falls outside drop zone visual at small viewport
findings.evidence: <div className="rounded-xl overflow-hidden bg-white dark:bg-[#1a1a1a] border-2 border-orange-400 dark:border-orange-500 shadow-[0_20px_60px_rgba(234,88,12,0.2)] w-[220px] rotate-[2deg] scale-105 pointer-events-none">
findings.user_impact: Drag overlay card is 220px wide × scaled 105% × rotated. On a 240px column (sm:min-w-[190px]), the overlay sometimes blocks the column header — user can't see which column they're hovering over.
findings.repro: Mobile board. Long-press a card. Drag overlay covers the next column's title.
findings.fix: Drop the rotation on mobile: `rotate-0 md:rotate-[2deg]`. Or scale down: `scale-100 md:scale-105`.

findings.title: Service worker registration in dangerouslySetInnerHTML — no cleanup or status indication
findings.evidence:
<script
  dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
    __html: `if("serviceWorker"in navigator){navigator.serviceWorker.register("/sw.js")}`,
  }}
/>
findings.user_impact: If `/sw.js` fails to load the registration throws silently in the console. No user notification that PWA install is unavailable. Users miss the 'install to home screen' prompt.
findings.repro: Visit smm.ten80ten.com. Open DevTools → Application → Service Workers. If sw.js 404s, no UI indication.
findings.fix: Wrap in try/catch and add a console.warn (or send to logging). Better: register in a `useEffect` in a layout client component instead of an inline script for cleanup on hot reload.

findings.title: Auth setup page: 'You're all set!' redirects in 2s — too fast for screen readers to announce
findings.user_impact: Success screen shows for 2 seconds then hard-redirects. Screen readers haven't finished announcing the success state. Slow mobile loads also see screen blink and then login screen — confusing for new users.
findings.repro: Complete invite setup. Watch screen flash 'You're all set!' then redirect.
findings.fix:
Extend timeout to 3000ms minimum. Add explicit aria-live: `
...
`. Also add a button: 'Continue to sign in' so users with motor disabilities can advance manually.

findings.title: Drag-and-drop touch sensor 200ms delay — users feel sluggish when starting drag on phone
findings.evidence: useSensor(TouchSensor, { activationConstraint: { delay: 200, tolerance: 5 } }),
findings.user_impact: 200ms long-press threshold + 5px tolerance means card feels 'stuck' before it picks up. Combined with the whole-card drag handle (UX-012), it's an awkward dance: tap = open card, hold 200ms = pickup. Users routinely think the drag failed and tap again.
findings.repro: Mobile. Try to drag a card. Feels delayed; sometimes opens drawer instead.
findings.fix: Reduce delay to 150ms and tolerance to 8 (more forgiving for shaky fingers). Better yet, with UX-012 fix (explicit drag handle), drop the delay to 0 — handle is intentional.

findings.title: Repurpose modal 'New Schedule *' input has tiny 28-unit time width — overflow on small screens
findings.evidence: setScheduledDate(e.target.value)} className="h-9 flex-1 ..." /> setScheduledTime(e.target.value)} className="h-9 w-28 ..." />
findings.user_impact: Time picker is fixed at w-28 (7rem = 112px). On iPhone SE (320px content) the iOS time picker `MM/DD AM` format doesn't fit; it shows '..AM' truncated. User can't verify the time they picked.
findings.repro: iPhone SE → Archive → Repurpose → set time. Display is truncated.
findings.fix: Use `w-[120px] sm:w-28` and let the date input take `flex-1`. Or stack vertically on mobile: `<div className="flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-2">` with full-width inputs.

findings.title: Validation error modal max-w-sm (384px) is too narrow for long error lists
findings.evidence: className="w-full max-w-sm sm:max-w-md max-h-[75vh] sm:max-h-[80vh] flex flex-col rounded-2xl overflow-hidden"
findings.user_impact: When 7+ fields are missing (which is common from Ideas → Awaiting Approval if user hasn't filled details), the list scrolls inside a 384px container. Each item's 'description' text wraps to 3-4 lines, making the list feel infinite.
findings.repro: Create a card in Ideas with no detail. Try to move to Awaiting Approval. See validation modal with 8 missing fields.
findings.fix: Bump to `max-w-md sm:max-w-lg` (448→512px) so each list item gets more breathing room. Title remains compact.

findings.title: Footer of asset-review-drawer not sticky on mobile — primary 'Approve' / 'Move to' buttons require scroll
findings.evidence:
<div className="px-6 py-4 border-t border-gray-100 dark:border-white/[0.06] shrink-0 space-y-2.5 bg-white dark:bg-[#0e0e11]">
  ...
  <Button size="sm" onClick={() => { ... }} className="flex-1 h-9 rounded-lg bg-emerald-600 hover:bg-emerald-700 ...">
findings.user_impact: Project memory IRON LAW: 'sticky-CTA pattern' on mobile. The drawer's primary action (Approve, Move to X, Submit Revision) sits at the bottom inside the scroll container. While drawer parent is `flex flex-col` and footer is shrink-0, on very tall content it works — but with mobile keyboard open (mention textarea), the footer slides up unpredictably.
findings.repro: Mobile. Open card → scroll to bottom → focus note textarea. Approve button intermittently visible/hidden.
findings.fix: Add explicit sticky positioning: `<div className="sticky bottom-0 px-6 py-4 border-t ... bg-white dark:bg-[#0e0e11] pb-[max(env(safe-area-inset-bottom),1rem)]">`. Safe-area-inset for iPhone home indicator.

findings.title: Studio rows use 12px input + 8.5px chip + 9.5px label — text too small to read or tap on mobile
findings.evidence:
{children}

...
findings.user_impact: Studio Card body uses 9.5px labels, 10px hints, 12px input text. On retina mobile this is barely legible. Combined with the dense 4-column grids (Date / Time / Platforms / Aspect on Row 1), the row feels like a spreadsheet from 2005.
findings.repro: Open Studio on iPhone. Try to read 'Style prompt' hint or 'Visual style' label.
findings.fix: Bump labels to `text-[10px] sm:text-[9.5px]`, inputs to `text-[13px] sm:text-[12px]`. Or stack the 4-col grid to 2-col on mobile: `grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-4`.

findings.title: Theme toggle button no announce — screen reader users don't know the new state
findings.evidence:
<button
  onClick={toggleTheme}
  aria-label={theme === "light" ? "Switch to dark mode" : "Switch to light mode"}
  className="p-2 rounded-lg ..."
  title={...}
>
  {theme === "light" ? <Moon className="w-4 h-4" aria-hidden="true" /> : <Sun className="w-4 h-4" aria-hidden="true" />}
</button>
findings.user_impact: Aria-label switches to the OPPOSITE action (good!), but there's no aria-live announce when the theme actually changes. Screen reader users hear 'Switch to dark mode' on focus, click, but don't get confirmation the page is now dark.
findings.repro: VoiceOver on. Tap theme toggle. Hear focus label change. No 'Dark mode enabled' announcement.
findings.fix:
Add `
{theme === 'light' ? 'Light mode enabled' : 'Dark mode enabled'}
` somewhere in the app shell. Or use `aria-pressed={theme === 'dark'}` to make it a toggle button.

findings.title: Many icon-only buttons missing aria-labels — only 11 across the codebase per grep
findings.evidence:
<button onClick={() => { if (confirm("Delete this post?")) { ... }} className="..." title="Delete"><Trash2 className="w-3.5 h-3.5" /></button>
<button onClick={closeDrawer} className="..."><X className="w-4 h-4" /></button>
findings.user_impact: Across kickback-modal, create-post-modal, revision-modal, repurpose-modal, avatar-crop-modal, asset-review-drawer — dozens of icon-only close (X), delete (Trash2), upload (Upload), and remove (X) buttons rely on `title="..."` which screen readers don't announce reliably. Project rule: 'icon-only buttons get aria-labels'.
findings.repro: VoiceOver. Tab through the asset review drawer header. Hear 'button' for both Trash and Close, no distinction.
findings.fix: Sweep: every icon-only `<button>` gets `aria-label="<verb> <noun>"`. Examples: `aria-label="Delete post"` on the trash, `aria-label="Close drawer"` on the X. Add `aria-hidden="true"` to the inner Lucide icon. Create a `<IconButton>` primitive that requires `label` prop.

findings.title: Skeleton card animate-pulse on dark mode is barely visible (white/[0.06])
findings.evidence:
<div className="w-full h-[76px] bg-gray-100 dark:bg-white/[0.06]" />
<div className="h-2.5 rounded bg-gray-100 dark:bg-white/[0.06] w-3/4" />
findings.user_impact: Dark mode skeleton uses `white/[0.06]` against `bg-[#1a1a1a]` — pulses between 6% and ~3% opacity, almost imperceptible. Users on slow connections in dark mode see what looks like empty boxes.
findings.repro: Dark mode + Slow 3G. Open Content Engine. Watch skeletons — barely visible.
findings.fix: Bump to `dark:bg-white/[0.1]` for the image area and `dark:bg-white/[0.08]` for the bars. Animation is harder; consider `animate-shimmer` instead of `animate-pulse` for better visibility.

findings.title: Lightbox 'fixed inset-0' but inner div uses pointer-events-none — user can't click image to dismiss when wrapper is the close target
findings.evidence:
<div onClick={() => setShowLightbox(false)} className="fixed inset-0 bg-black/80 z-[60] backdrop-blur-sm cursor-pointer" />
<div className="fixed inset-0 z-[60] flex items-center justify-center p-4 sm:p-8 pointer-events-none">
findings.user_impact: Lightbox dismisses by clicking the backdrop (good!), but the inner wrapper sets `pointer-events-none` and only the inner div has `pointer-events-auto`. The image itself is inside the auto wrapper, so clicking image does NOT dismiss (right behavior) but clicking just outside the image still hits the inner div. Confusing for users who expect 'click anywhere on the dim area = dismiss'.
findings.repro: Open card with thumbnail → click thumbnail → lightbox opens → click empty area near the image (between image edge and viewport edge). Sometimes dismisses, sometimes not.
findings.fix: Move the close handler onto the outer flex container too: `<div onClick={(e) => { if (e.target === e.currentTarget) setShowLightbox(false); }} className="fixed inset-0 z-[60] flex items-center justify-center p-4 sm:p-8 cursor-pointer">` then the image+close-button live inside without pointer-events trick. Also add Escape handler.

findings.title: Studio card 'Cancel' button only appears when status is 'generating' — no way to undo Generate misclick
findings.evidence:
{row.status === "generating" || row.status === "revising" ? (
  <Button size="sm" variant="outline" onClick={onCancel} ...><X className="w-3 h-3 mr-1" />Cancel</Button>
) : (
  <Button size="sm" onClick={onGenerate} disabled={!canGenerate || busy} ...>
findings.user_impact: Generate kicks off a server job. Cancel button only appears after the job has registered as 'generating'. Window between 'I clicked Generate' and 'job state = generating' has no cancel. With a $3/row cap, accidental clicks cost.
findings.repro: Click Generate on a misconfigured row. The button immediately disables while loading. No way to cancel during the 2-3s creation window. By the time Cancel appears, the AI request is already submitted.
findings.fix: Show Cancel button immediately on `busy=true` (after click but before status update). Wire optimistic cancellation: keep a `cancelRequestedRef` and check it before the server-side fetch resolves.

findings.title: Schedule input pair uses native date+time inputs with no min — accepts past dates silently
findings.evidence: setScheduledDate(e.target.value)} className={`${inputClass} flex-[3]`} required /> setScheduledTime(e.target.value)} className={`${inputClass} flex-[2]`} required />
findings.user_impact: User can schedule a post for last week, which is meaningless. Project has timezone awareness (CST in repurpose-modal, workspace-tz in settings) but no min-date guard in the modal. Worse, the schedule input in the drawer (line 420) also has no min.
findings.repro: Create Post → set date 2024-01-01. Submit. Card created with past date. n8n cron will skip.
findings.fix: Add `min={new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10)}` to all `<input type="date">`. For time, if date == today, set `min={current time HH:MM}`. Show inline error: 'Pick a future date/time'.
